name: Ammar ibn Hassan
title: The Valiant of Tai
shortDescription: A sincere and brave Shia from Kufa whose father was martyred in the Battle of Jamal, and who joined the Imam in Karbala.
fullStory:
Ammar ibn Hassan al-Ta'i was a sincere Shia and a well-known brave warrior of Kufa. He was raised in a family where love for Wilayah was deeply rooted; his father, Hassan ibn Shurayh, fought alongside Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) in the Battle of Jamal and was martyred.

When Imam Hussain (AS) moved towards Karbala, Ammar, along with a group of Kufan Shias, secretly left the city at night, evading Ibn Ziyad's spies, and reached the Imam's caravan. During the days of the siege, he was one of the Imam's firm and steadfast companions, encouraging others with his words.

On the day of Ashura, Ammar bravely entered the battlefield during the first mass attack by Umar Sa'd's army, which began before noon. He struck the enemy ranks with his sword and defended the sanctity of the Ahl al-Bayt despite the rain of arrows.

In this cowardly attack, where many of Imam Hussain's (AS) companions were martyred, Ammar ibn Hassan also, after a long and exhausting fight, was targeted by enemy arrows and spears, and his pure body fell to the ground.

His martyrdom demonstrated the continuation of the red line of martyrdom in authentic Shia families; a father martyred alongside Ali (AS) and a son sacrificing his life alongside Hussain (AS). His name is also mentioned with greetings and peace from the infallible Imam in the Ziyarat of Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عَمّارِ بْنِ حَسّانَ الطّائِي...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Ammar ibn Hassan al-Ta'i.
